ops.title: User-global Claude config edits must sweep every cc-pool account copy, not just ~/.claude.json
ops.body:
cc-pool provisions each account dir (`~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-NN/` and `acct-NN.private/`) with its own **copy** of `.claude.json`, and a `settings.json` that is sometimes a symlink to `~/.claude/settings.json` and sometimes a divergent real copy. `claude mcp add/remove` edits only the **active** account's `.claude.json` (via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR), so a "user-global" config change silently misses the canonical file and every other account.

Recipe for a global change: edit `~/.claude.json` **plus** loop `/Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/*/.claude.json` with jq (write to a tmp file then `cat tmp > f` — plain `mv` would drop the 600 perms); for `settings.json` skip symlinks (`[ -L ]`) and patch only real files. Verified live 2026-07-12 during the cc-context MCP dedup: 27 `.claude.json` removals + 13 `settings.json` permission swaps. Related: [[shared-working-copy-concurrency]], [[uv-exclude-newer-blocks-fresh-releases]].
